title: 10 best herbal creams for nipple pain during breastfeeding

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Calendula, also known as pot marigold, has anti-inflammatory compounds like quercetin and kaempferol that help reduce swelling and ease discomfort in the nipples, making it an effective natural remedy for breastfeeding mothers.

Antimicrobial properties: The antiseptic and antibacterial properties of calendula help prevent infections and promote wound healing by creating a protective barrier against bacteria and other microorganisms, reducing the risk of infection and promoting nipple health.

Soothing and emollient properties: Calendula's emollient and soothing qualities help to moisturize and calm irritated or damaged nipples, reducing pain, discomfort, and inflammation associated with breastfeeding difficulties.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis, also known as pot marigold, creams helps with nipple pain during breastfeeding because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The cream's active compounds, such as triterpenoids and carotenoids, help to reduce swelling and irritation, promoting faster healing of cracked and sore nipples. Calendula's natural antiseptic properties also prevent infection and promote a healthy environment for nipple recovery, allowing mothers to continue breastfeeding comfortably and with confidence. This natural remedy provides instant relief and comfort to nursing mothers.
remedy_constituents: Beta-sitosterol: This triterpenoid helps reduce inflammation and promotes tissue repair, which can alleviate nipple soreness and discomfort associated with breastfeeding.
Quercetin: As a flavonoid phenolic compound, quercetin exhibits potent anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe and calm irritated nipples, reducing pain and discomfort.

N-caffeoyltyramine: This phenylethanoid has been shown to exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities, which can help prevent or reduce the severity of nipple thrush and other infections that may contribute to breastfeeding-related nipple pain.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used due to their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that help to reduce nipple soreness and discomfort.
Leaves: They are used due to their astringent properties that help to reduce swelling and inflammation of the nipples.
Stems: They are used due to their anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe and calm irritated nipples.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 calendula flowers at peak bloom to ensure optimal potency and color.
Dry the flowers in a warm place with good air circulation for 1-2 weeks.
Grind 2 ounces of dried flowers into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar.
Mix 1 teaspoon of the powder with 2 tablespoons of distilled water to create a strong infusion.
Combine the infusion with 1/2 cup of emulsifying wax, 1/4 cup of beeswax, and 1/4 cup of coconut oil to create a rich cream.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce swelling and inflammation on the nipples, providing relief from painful engorgement and soreness.
Antioxidant properties: Aloe vera's antioxidants neutralize free radicals, which can damage breast tissue and cause nipple pain, promoting healthy skin and reduced discomfort.

Wound-healing properties: Aloe vera's gel accelerates wound healing by promoting collagen synthesis, improving blood flow, and reducing bacterial growth, which helps to soothe cracked nipples and alleviate pain.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe vera, also known as aloe, creams helps with nipple pain during breastfeeding because of its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. The gel extracted from aloe vera plants contains compounds that reduce swelling and discomfort, making it an effective natural remedy for nipple soreness. Additionally, aloe vera creams promote wound healing and tissue repair, which can help alleviate nipple trauma caused by breastfeeding. This makes aloe vera creams a popular and comforting solution for nursing mothers experiencing nipple pain.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: These complex compounds help to reduce inflammation and soothe irritated skin, which can contribute to nipple pain during breastfeeding.
Glycoproteins (Acemannan): Acemannan has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that may help to calm nipple soreness and promote healing of nipple tissues.

Volatile oils (including Furanocoumarins): These oils have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties that may help to prevent and treat nipple infections (such as thrush) and associated pain, which is common during breastfeeding.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain a high concentration of aloe vera gel, which is often extracted and used to soothe and moisturize the nipples.
Gel from the Leaves: It has anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that help to reduce pain, swelling, and infection.
Aloe Vera Extract from the Leaves: It can be used to create creams that provide long-lasting relief from nipple pain and promote wound healing.
remedy_recipe: Gather and weigh out ingredients including 2 ounces of aloe vera gel and 1 ounce of coconut oil.
Melt the coconut oil in a double boiler over low heat for about 10 minutes.
Combine the aloe vera gel with 1 teaspoon of vitamin e oil and 1 teaspoon of beeswax in a small bowl.
Heat the aloe vera mixture over low heat for about 5 minutes or until the beeswax is fully dissolved.
Whip the mixture with a hand mixer until it has doubled in volume and is smooth and creamy.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Purple coneflower's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as kaempferol and quercetin, help to reduce inflammation and alleviate nipple soreness and pain caused by breastfeeding.

Antimicrobial properties: The herb's antimicrobial properties, including salicin and isorhapontigenin, help to prevent bacterial and fungal infections that can cause nipple pain and discomfort during breastfeeding.

Anesthetic and analgesic properties: Purple coneflower contains compounds like ursolic acid and oleanolic acid, which have been shown to have anesthetic and analgesic effects, helping to numb and relieve nipple pain and discomfort.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea, also known as purple coneflower, creams helps with nipple pain during breastfeeding because of its anti-inflammatory properties. The cream reduces swelling and redness associated with nipple irritation, making it a popular remedy among breastfeeding mothers. Additionally, Echinacea purpurea's antimicrobial properties help to combat infections and promote tissue healing, providing relief from sore nipples and promoting a smooth breastfeeding experience. This natural remedy supports the body's natural healing process, making it an attractive solution for nursing mothers.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid glycosides: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and discomfort in the nipples, thus alleviating nipple pain.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids are known for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe and calm irritated nipples, thereby reducing pain and discomfort.
Alkylamides: Alkylamides have anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, which can help reduce nipple inflammation and promote healing, leading to a decrease in nipple pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in inulin, which is believed to have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe nipple pain.
Leaves: Contain alkaloids that may help reduce inflammation and ease discomfort associated with nipple pain.
Flowers: Used for their potential antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help prevent infection and reduce pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest echinacea purpurea flowers and roots in the summer months when they are in full bloom and dry them immediately.
Combine 2 tablespoons of dried echinacea flowers and 1 tablespoon of dried echinacea roots in a clean glass jar.
Infuse the dried echinacea mixture in 2 cups of boiling water for 10 to 15 minutes to create a strong tea.
Strain the tea and mix it with 1/2 cup of beeswax and 1/2 cup of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Whip the mixture until it thickens and add a few drops of vitamin e oil to preserve the cream and store it in a cool place.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Chickweed cream's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and irritation in the breast tissue, which can contribute to nipple soreness and pain.

Antibacterial and antifungal: The antimicrobial properties of chickweed cream prevent infections and promote healing by reducing the growth of bacteria and fungi that can cause nipple thrush and other infections.

Emollient and soothing: Chickweed's emollient and soothing properties help moisturize and calm the nipples, providing relief from dryness, cracking, and irritation that can cause pain during breastfeeding.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stellaria media, also known as chickweed, creams helps with nipple pain during breastfeeding because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The cream is derived from the plant's leaves and flowers, which contain flavonoids and saponins that work to reduce inflammation and irritation. This natural remedy helps to calm and heal the nipple area, providing relief from soreness and discomfort associated with breastfeeding. The cream can also help to promote a healthy environment for skin regeneration, allowing mothers to nurse their babies with ease and comfort.
remedy_constituents: Triterpenoids: These compounds exhibit anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and irritation in the nipple area, alleviating pain and discomfort.
Flavonoids: As antioxidants, flavonoids help protect the nipple tissue from oxidative stress and inflammation, promoting healing and reducing pain.

Coumarins: These compounds have been shown to exhibit analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help numb the pain and reduce inflammation in the nipple area, making them a potential solution for breastfeeding-related nipple pain.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Stellaria media leaves are used due to their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that help to calm nipple pain and irritation.
Roots: Stellaria media roots are used because they contain compounds with anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which help to reduce nipple pain and discomfort.
Flowers: Stellaria media flowers are used due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help to soothe and protect the nipples from irritation and infection.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 stellaria media flowers and leaves on a dry sunny morning to ensure maximum potency.
Steep 10g of stellaria media flowers and leaves in 100ml of boiling water for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the stellaria media infusion through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.
Mix 20g of beeswax and 20g of shea butter with the cooled stellaria media infusion in a double boiler.
Whip the stellaria media cream mixture until it thickens and becomes smooth and creamy texture.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Plantain creams containing anti-inflammatory compounds, such as allantoin, can reduce swelling and redness caused by cracked or sore nipples, providing instant relief from discomfort.

Antibacterial: The antibacterial properties of plantain, particularly berberine, can help prevent infection and promote healing by controlling the growth of bacteria that may exacerbate nipple pain and damage.

Soothing and calming: Plantain's natural soothing and calming effects can quickly ease nipple tenderness and discomfort, making it an effective natural remedy for nursing mothers experiencing sore or irritated nipples.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Plantago major, also known as plantain, creams helps with nipple pain during breastfeeding because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The plantain plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine to treat various skin irritations and wounds. The cream derived from it is thought to calm and reduce inflammation in the nipple, providing relief from the pain and discomfort associated with engorgement, nipple thrush, and other breastfeeding-related issues. This natural remedy offers a gentle and effective solution for nursing mothers.
remedy_constituents: Apolypodin: This sesquiterpene lactone has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help to reduce nipple pain and discomfort during breastfeeding.

Phenolic acids (specifically, ferulic acid and sinapic acid): These compounds exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, which can help to soothe and protect the nipple tissue, reducing pain and inflammation associated with breastfeeding.

Flavonoids (specifically, quercetin and kaempferol): These plant-derived compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to alleviate nipple pain and discomfort by reducing inflammation and promoting tissue healing.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the most commonly used part for creams to relieve nipple pain during breastfeeding due to their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties.
Seeds: Plantago major seeds are used for their mucilaginous properties, which help to soothe and protect the nipple area.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30g of dried plantago major leaves and flowers in late summer or early autumn season.
Mix the dried plant material with 100ml of oil such as sunflower or sweet almond oil.
Infuse the mixture in a double boiler for 2 hours at a temperature of 40-60 degrees celsius.
Strain the infused mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container.
Whip the infused oil mixture with 10g of beeswax until it reaches a smooth and creamy consistency.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: St John's Wort cream reduces inflammation and swelling on the nipples, which can cause pain and discomfort during breastfeeding.

Antibacterial properties: St John's Wort cream has antimicrobial properties that help to prevent bacterial infections, which can cause nipple pain and thrush, a common condition in breastfeeding mothers.

Sedative and analgesic properties: St John's Wort cream contains sedative and analgesic compounds that help to numb the area and reduce nerve sensitivity, providing quick relief from nipple soreness and pain during breastfeeding.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum, also known as St John's Wort, creams helps with nipple pain during breastfeeding because of its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The cream's active compounds, including hyperforin and hypericin, have been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain in the nipple area, making it a popular natural remedy among breastfeeding mothers. Regular application can help to soothe and protect the nipple tissue, promoting comfort and confidence for both mother and baby.
remedy_constituents: Hypericin: This naphthodianthrone alkaloid has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help to reduce pain and discomfort associated with nipple soreness during breastfeeding.

Flavonoids (especially rutin and quercetin): These phenolic compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help to alleviate nipple pain by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress.

Nordohydrone: This phenolic compound has been reported to have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which may contribute to the pain-relieving effects of Hypericum perforatum creams on nipple pain during breastfeeding.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in hypericin and hyperforin, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that help soothe nipple pain.
Flowers: They contain flavonoids and glycosides, which contribute to the antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects that can help alleviate nipple soreness.
Roots: They are a source of hypericin and hyperforin, which, when extracted, are used to create creams that reduce inflammation and provide pain relief for breastfeeding mothers.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-20g of dried hypericum perforatum flowers and leaves from a trusted source.
Combine the dried hypericum perforatum with 50g of beeswax and 50g of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat, stirring occasionally, for 20-30 minutes or until the beeswax has melted.
Add 20g of shea butter and 10g of vitamin e oil to the mixture and stir until well combined.
Pour the mixture into a clean glass jar and let it cool and solidify for 30-40 minutes.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Comfrey's allantoin and rosmarinic acid components reduce inflammation, swelling, and redness, providing relief from painful nipples.
Wound healing properties: Comfrey's glycosides and flavonoids accelerate wound healing by promoting collagen synthesis, cell growth, and tissue repair, helping to soothe and protect sore nipples.
Soothing and calming effects: Comfrey's triterpenoid saponins have a calming effect on the nervous system, reducing anxiety, stress, and discomfort associated with nipple pain during breastfeeding.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Symphytum officinale, also known as comfrey, creams helps with nipple pain during breastfeeding because it contains anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that soothe and calm the nipples. The cream's ability to reduce swelling and promote healing makes it an effective remedy for nipple soreness and cracking. Comfrey creams are also rich in vitamins and minerals that promote tissue repair and regeneration, allowing mothers to breastfeed comfortably and confidently. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to alleviate nipple pain, making it a trusted solution for breastfeeding mothers.
remedy_constituents: Allantoin: Helps with nipple pain by accelerating wound healing and reducing inflammation, which is beneficial for sore nipples caused by breastfeeding.
Glucosinolates: Contribute to the anti-inflammatory properties of the cream, reducing pain and discomfort associated with nipple soreness during breastfeeding.
Saponins: Have anti-inflammatory and soothing effects, which can help to calm and protect the nipple tissue, reducing pain and discomfort during breastfeeding.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their high mucilage content, which provides soothing and protective properties to the nipple.
Roots: Used due to their high concentration of anti-inflammatory compounds, which help to reduce nipple pain and inflammation.
Stems: Used due to their similar composition to the leaves, containing mucilage that helps to soothe and protect the nipple.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 25g of fresh symphytum officinale leaves and flowers, clean them thoroughly with distilled water.
Infuse 10g of the harvested plant material in 100ml of hot distilled water for 5 minutes.
Strain the infused liquid through cheesecloth, collect the liquid and discard the solids immediately.
Mix 30g of beeswax with 20g of candelilla wax and melt them in a double boiler at 180f.
Combine the infused liquid with 10g of vitamin e oil and the melted wax mixture, stir well until combined.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Stinging nettle cream's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce swelling and inflammation in the breast tissue, which may contribute to nipple pain during breastfeeding.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in stinging nettle cream can help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate nipple soreness and discomfort during breastfeeding.

Hormone-regulating properties: Stinging nettle has been shown to have a regulatory effect on hormone levels, including prolactin, which is essential for lactation. This may help alleviate nipple pain caused by hormonal imbalances or fluctuations.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Urtica dioica, also known as stinging nettle, creams helps with nipple pain during breastfeeding because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The cream's active compounds, such as flavonoids and saponins, work to reduce inflammation and irritation in the nipple area, providing relief from pain, soreness, and discomfort. As a result, nursing mothers can experience reduced pain and increased comfort during breastfeeding, allowing them to nurse their babies without distraction or discomfort. This natural remedy can be especially beneficial for new mothers experiencing nipple soreness.
remedy_constituents: Sterols: Sterols, particularly beta-sitosterol, have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation and pain associated with nipple soreness during breastfeeding.

Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids, such as ferulic acid and caffeic acid, have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help soothe and heal damaged nipple tissue, reducing pain and discomfort.

Saponins: Saponins, particularly ursolic acid, have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that can help alleviate pain and discomfort associated with nipple soreness, promoting healing and reducing inflammation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Urtica dioica leaves are the most commonly used part in nipple creams to soothe and calm the skin, reducing pain and inflammation associated with breastfeeding.
Roots: Urtica dioica roots are also used due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and pain in the nipple area.
Stems: Some products use Urtica dioica stems, which contain similar properties to the leaves and roots, providing relief from nipple pain and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest urtica dioica leaves and stems in late spring or early summer when they are at their highest potency.
Dry the harvested plant material at 40c for 2 hours to remove excess moisture and prevent spoilage.
Create an infusion by steeping 30 grams of dried urtica dioica in 1 liter of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the infusion using a cheesecloth to separate the plant material from the oil and discard the solids.
Mix the infused oil with a thickening agent like beeswax to create a creamy texture suitable for topical application.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Wild strawberry creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like salicylic acid, which can help reduce swelling and discomfort in sore nipples, providing relief from pain.

Antibacterial and antifungal properties: The cream's antimicrobial properties can help prevent infections that may cause nipple pain by inhibiting the growth of bacteria and fungi, promoting a healthy breast tissue environment.

Emollient and soothing properties: Wild strawberry creams typically contain moisturizing ingredients like glycerin and lanolin, which can help soften and calm irritated skin, reducing discomfort and soreness associated with breastfeeding.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fragaria vesca, also known as wild strawberry, creams helps with nipple pain during breastfeeding because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The cream's active compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, help to calm and reduce inflammation in the nipple area, providing relief from discomfort and pain. Additionally, the cream's moisturizing properties help to keep the nipples hydrated and healthy, promoting a smooth and pain-free breastfeeding experience. This natural remedy can be a welcome relief for new mothers.
remedy_constituents: Triterpenoids: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and discomfort in the nipples, making them a valuable constituent in soothing nipple pain.

Ellagic acid: A polyphenolic compound with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, ellagic acid can help alleviate nipple soreness and promote wound healing, reducing the risk of nipple trauma during breastfeeding.
Anthocyanins: These powerful antioxidants have anti-inflammatory properties and may help protect the nipples from oxidative stress, promoting healing and reducing pain associated with breastfeeding.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in salicylic acid and other anti-inflammatory compounds, which help reduce swelling and ease pain.
Fruits: Containing fragaria, a glycoside with potential anti-inflammatory properties that may help alleviate nipple pain.
Roots: Acting as a natural analgesic, roots of Fragaria vesca may help numb the area and provide relief from nipple pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fragaria vesca flowers and leaves in the morning after dew has evaporated for best quality.
Steep 1 cup of fragaria vesca flowers in 2 cups of boiling water for 5 minutes to make an infusion.
Strain the infusion through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container to remove solids.
Combine 1/2 cup of shea butter and 1/4 cup of beeswax in a small saucepan over low heat.
Mix 2 tablespoons of the fragaria vesca infusion with 2 tablespoons of aloe vera gel and 2 tablespoons of vitamin e oil.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Dandelion cream's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and terpenes, help reduce swelling and discomfort in the nipple area, alleviating pain associated with mastitis or nipple thrush.

Antimicrobial properties: The antimicrobial components of dandelion cream, including triterpenoid saponins and phenolic acids, combat bacterial or fungal infections that can cause nipple pain and inflammation, promoting a healthy breast environment.

Soothing and emollient properties: Dandelion's moisturizing and nourishing compounds, such as vitamins A, C, and E, and fatty acids, help soothe and hydrate the nipple area, reducing irritation and discomfort caused by friction or chafing.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Taraxacum officinale, also known as dandelion, creams helps with nipple pain during breastfeeding because of its anti-inflammatory properties. The cream's active ingredients, such as taraxasterol and inulin, work to soothe and calm irritated nipples, reducing discomfort and pain. Additionally, Taraxacum officinale is said to promote wound healing and prevent infection, which can be beneficial for mothers experiencing nipple trauma or cracks during breastfeeding. By providing relief and promoting healing, Taraxacum officinale creams can help support a mother's ability to nurse comfortably.
remedy_constituents: Inulin: Inulin, a fructan polysaccharide, helps reduce inflammation and soothe irritated skin, providing relief from nipple pain during breastfeeding.
Luteolin: Luteolin, a flavonoid phenolic compound, exhibits anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate nipple soreness and discomfort associated with breastfeeding.

Taraxasterol: Taraxasterol, a triterpene saponin, has been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and wound-healing properties, which may aid in the recovery of nipple skin and reduce pain associated with breastfeeding.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Taraxacum officinale are used due to their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which help to reduce nipple pain and discomfort.
Flowers: The flowers of Taraxacum officinale are used for their calming effect, which helps to reduce nipple soreness and promote healing.
Roots: The roots of Taraxacum officinale are used for their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help to reduce nipple pain, prevent infection, and promote healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of taraxacum officinale flowers and leaves from a clean area at dawn.
Steam distill the plant material for 20 minutes to extract the essential oil.
Combine 50g of beeswax and 25g of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Mix 10ml of the extracted taraxacum officinale essential oil with the beeswax mixture.
Stir the mixture for 10 minutes and pour it into jars for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender creams helps with nipple pain during breastfeeding because they provide a soothing and calming effect on the nipple tissue. The anti-inflammatory properties of lavender help to reduce redness, swelling, and discomfort caused by friction or latch issues. Additionally, the topical application of lavender cream can also aid in promoting wound healing and reducing the risk of nipple trauma, allowing mothers to feed comfortably and enjoy the bonding experience with their babies.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic property: Helps to relax the muscle fibers surrounding the nipples, reducing spasms and discomfort caused by latch issues or poor latching.
Anti-inflammatory property: Reduces swelling and redness in the nipple area, which can occur as a result of frequent feeding, irritation from baby's tongue or mouth, or other causes.
Anxiolytic property: Calms nervous tension and promotes relaxation, helping to reduce stress and anxiety that can exacerbate nipple pain during breastfeeding.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain a high concentration of essential oils, which provide anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them effective in reducing nipple pain.
Flowers: Lavender flowers are rich in linalool and linalyl acetate, two compounds that have been shown to reduce inflammation and ease discomfort associated with nipple pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried lavender flowers measuring 2 tablespoons and grind them into fine powder using mortar and pestle.
Combine the powdered lavender with sweet almond oil in a double boiler at 160°f for 30 minutes to create an infused oil.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle and discard solids retaining 4 ounces of oil.
Mix the strained oil with beeswax pellets measuring 2 tablespoons and cocoa butter measuring 1 tablespoon in a double boiler at 180°f for 10 minutes.
Pour the mixture into sterilized glass containers and let it cool to room temperature before use taking approximately 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm creams helps with nipple pain during breastfeeding because its soothing properties calm and comfort sensitive nipples. The anti-inflammatory compounds in lemon balm reduce swelling and ease discomfort, making it an effective natural remedy for sore nipples. Additionally, lemon balm's antispasmodic properties help to relax the muscles in the breast, allowing for smoother milk flow and reduced nipple pain. This results in a more comfortable breastfeeding experience for both mom and baby.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic property: Lemon balm's antispasmodic properties help to relax and soothe sore and irritated nipples, reducing muscle spasms and discomfort caused by breastfeeding.
Anti-inflammatory property: The anti-inflammatory compounds in lemon balm creams can reduce swelling and redness of the nipples, making them feel softer and more comfortable during feeding sessions.
Anxiolytic (anti-anxiety) property: Lemon balm's calming effects can help to alleviate stress and anxiety related to nipple pain, promoting a sense of relaxation and calmness for both mom and baby.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain rosmarinic acid, a compound that has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which can help soothe and relieve nipple pain during breastfeeding.
Leaves: Lemon balm leaves have been traditionally used to treat nipple soreness due to their antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can reduce discomfort and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried melissa officinalis flowers and grind into fine powder using mortar and pestle.
Mix ground melissa officinalis flowers with sweet almond oil at a ratio of 10:1 for optimal potency.
Combine the mixture in a double boiler and heat gently over low heat for 30 minutes to infuse oil with herbal properties.
Remove from heat and let cool, then strain through cheesecloth into a clean glass container for storage.
Whip cream by mixing cooled infused oil with beeswax at a ratio of 20:1 and heating until melted, then pouring into prepared containers for use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Joe pye weed creams helps with nipple pain during breastfeeding because they contain compounds that have natural anti-inflammatory properties. The cream's active ingredients, such as salicylic acid and rosin, help to reduce swelling and discomfort in the nipple area, making it a soothing solution for nursing mothers. As a result, the nipple pain associated with breastfeeding is alleviated, allowing mothers to continue feeding their babies comfortably and without disruption.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: The plant contains flavonoids and polyphenolic acids that have anti-inflammatory properties, which could help reduce swelling and discomfort in the nipples.

Antibacterial and antifungal properties: Joe Pye Weed has been shown to exhibit antimicrobial activity against various bacteria and fungi, which could help prevent infections that may contribute to nipple pain during breastfeeding.
Wound-healing compounds: The plant contains triterpenoids and lignans with potential wound-healing and tissue-regenerating properties, which could aid in the healing of sore or cracked nipples.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making them effective in reducing redness and discomfort caused by nipple soreness.
Barks: The barks contain compounds that help soothe and calm irritated skin, providing relief from nipple pain and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh eupatorium perfoliatum flowers and leaves and rinse them thoroughly under cold running water.
Chop the herb into small pieces and combine with 1 cup of carrier oil such as coconut or sweet almond oil.
Allow the mixture to infuse for at least 2 hours in a cool dark place before straining and discarding solids.
Mix the infused oil with 10% beeswax and heat until melted then whip until smooth and creamy consistency is achieved.
Pour the herbal cream into sterilized containers and store in the refrigerator for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hawthorn creams helps with nipple pain during breastfeeding because they contain anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm irritated nipples. The cream's natural ingredients, such as hawthorn berries, richly nourish and protect the skin, reducing redness and discomfort. Additionally, hawthorn's antispasmodic effects help to relax the breast tissue, easing tension and pain caused by engorgement or poor latching. By providing a comforting barrier between the nipple and baby's mouth, hawthorn creams offer gentle relief from nipple soreness, allowing breastfeeding mothers to enjoy a more comfortable and satisfying feeding experience.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant Properties: Hawthorn's antioxidants help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which can contribute to nipple soreness and discomfort, allowing for a more comfortable breastfeeding experience.

Anti-inflammatory Compounds: Hawthorn's flavonoids and polyphenols have anti-inflammatory effects that soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness, swelling, and pain associated with nipple thrush or other forms of nipple pain.

Soothing and Calming Effects: The gentle, non-irritating properties of hawthorn cream can provide immediate relief from discomfort, allowing mothers to breastfeed without pain or anxiety, promoting a more positive experience.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which help soothe and calm irritated nipples.
Buds: Contain flavonoids and anthocyanins that have anti-inflammatory properties, reducing swelling and discomfort associated with nipple pain.
remedy_recipe: Gather 20g of dried crataegus monogyna flowers and 10ml of carrier oil such as coconut or sweet almond oil.
Combine the dried herbs in a small bowl and carefully pour the carrier oil over them, allowing for a 1:5 ratio of herb to oil.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler or a heat-proof glass bowl set over simmering water for 20-30 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container, pressing on the solids to extract as much oil as possible.
Allow the cream to cool and thicken at room temperature before transferring it to an airtight container for storage and use within 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Agrimony creams helps with nipple pain during breastfeeding because they provide a soothing, natural barrier that cushions the nipples and reduces friction caused by infant suction. remedy_recipe: Harvest agrimonia eupatoria leaves and flowers, ensuring fresh and clean materials for optimal potency.
Combine 30g of dried agrimonia eupatoria with 200ml of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil or coconut oil in a double boiler.
Steep the mixture for 4-6 hours under low heat, allowing the herbal extract to infuse into the oil.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth and transfer it to a clean glass jar, discarding the solids and reserving the infused oil.
Mix the infused oil with 10% beeswax and 1% vitamin e oil, heating the blend gently until the wax is fully incorporated and the cream has a smooth consistency.

remedy_recipe: Harvest galium aparine roots and dried them according to traditional methods for at least six months.
Grind 20 grams of dried galium aparine root into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine the powdered root with 50 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler and heat it gently over low heat for two hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter to remove any remaining plant material and discard the solids.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh petasites hybridus leaves and chop them into small pieces, ensuring you have 100g of dried material for the recipe.
Combine the chopped petasites hybridus with 500ml of olive oil in a glass jar, allowing it to infuse for 4 weeks under sunlight.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter, squeezing out as much liquid as possible from the herb residue, retaining 250ml of infused oil.
Mix the infused oil with 20g of beeswax and 10g of coconut oil in a double boiler, heating the mixture until it reaches a temperature of 120°f.
Pour the cooled cream into sterilized jars, allowing it to solidify before use within 6 weeks and storing any leftover cream in an airtight container for up to 3 months.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 1/4 cup of dried centaurea cyanus flowers and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine 2 tablespoons of coconut oil with 2 tablespoons of beeswax in a double boiler over low heat for 15 minutes.
Add 1 teaspoon of ground centaurea cyanus to the melted mixture and stir well to combine, allowing it to steep for an additional 10 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass bowl, discarding the solids and allowing the cream to cool completely.
Pour the cooled cream into sterilized jars, sealing them tightly and storing in the refrigerator for up to 6 months, ready to use as needed.

Heat the mixture at 60°c for 30 minutes while stirring occasionally to ensure thorough emulsification.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile creams helps with nipple pain during breastfeeding because of its soothing and calming properties. remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 cups of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers and grind them into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Mix the powdered matricaria chamomilla with 1 cup of coconut oil in a double boiler at medium heat for 30 minutes stirring occasionally.
Add 2 tablespoons of beeswax to the mixture and stir until melted and fully incorporated taking approximately 5-7 minutes.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool for 10-15 minutes before whipping with an electric mixer until light and fluffy taking about 5 minutes.
Transfer the cream to a sterilized container and store in the refrigerator for up to 6 months or freeze for longer storage allowing the cream to fully solidify at room temperature before use.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for nipple pain during breastfeeding are topical remedies that provide relief from discomfort, soreness, and inflammation of the nipple area, helping breastfeeding mothers to continue nursing their babies without pain or distress. Some of the best herbal creams for nipple pain during breastfeeding include Calendula officinalis, Aloe barbadensis, Arnica montana, Symphytum officinale, Lavandula angustifolia, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Urtica dioica, Chamomilla recutita, Hypericum perforatum, and Vitex agnus-castus. If left untreated, nipple pain can lead to frustration, anxiety, and even premature weaning, negatively impacting the mother-baby bond and causing emotional distress for both parties.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to nipple pain during breastfeeding that are treated with herbal creams are eczema, dermatitis, and psoriasis. Herbal creams containing ingredients like aloe vera, tea tree oil, and chamomile can provide relief from inflammation, itching, and discomfort associated with these conditions. These creams can help soothe and calm irritated skin, promoting healing and reducing pain, making them a popular natural alternative for treating various skin irritations.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with nipple pain during breastfeeding is a blend of comfrey, calendula, and arnica. Comfrey accelerates healing, while calendula soothes and reduces inflammation. Arnica provides pain relief and anti-inflammatory properties. Apply a thin layer of the combined cream to your nipples after each feeding, allowing it to absorb fully before dressing. This gentle, natural combination can help alleviate nipple soreness and promote comfortable breastfeeding. Use it regularly for optimal results.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Iranian journal of pharmaceutical research: IJPR, the application of Zizyphus Jujube lotion on nipple fissure showed promising results. In a double-blind clinical trial of 100 primiparous lactating women, the Jujube group, which used 0.5 mL of Zizyphus Jujube lotion, showed a significant difference in the extent of nipple damage compared to the control group, which applied breast milk, on the 7th and 14th days after childbirth. This indicates that herbal creams like Zizyphus Jujube lotion can be effective in healing nipple pain during breastfeeding, reducing the severity of nipple damage and sore nipple discharge.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis creams helps with nipple pain during breastfeeding because its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties soothe and protect the nipple area. remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 dried calendula flowers at the peak of potency and store them in an airtight container.
Combine 1 cup of dried calendula flowers with 2 cups of carrier oil in a clean glass jar.
Infuse the mixture for 2-3 weeks in a cool dark place with occasional shaking and stirring.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean bowl and discard the solids.
Mix 1/2 cup of the infused oil with 1/4 cup of beeswax and 1/4 cup of shea butter in a double boiler.

Strain the oil mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container to remove the plant material.
Add 10-20 grams of beeswax and 10-20 grams of shea butter to the strained oil mixture and heat gently until melted.
Pour the melted mixture into a clean tin or jar and allow it to cool and harden for about 30 minutes.
